rulings.Case Title: PRIYA SANGER et al VS. LEAH AHN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To: (1) Fix Amount Owed Pursuant To Arbitration Award, (2) Conirm Payment Of Award Under Protest, (3) Suspend Writ Of Execurtion, (4) Release Liens, And (5) For Such Other And Further Relief As The Court Deems Just And Equitable
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT LEAH AHN's Motion To: (1) Fix Amount Owed Pursuant To Arbitration Award, (2) Confirm Payment Of Award Under Protest, (3) Suspend Writ Of Execution, (4) Release Liens, And (5) For Such Other And Further Relief As The Court Deems Just And Equitable GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED regarding the request to fix the amount owe. The amount shall be fixed subject to rulings on other pending motions. Motion is otherwise DENIED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA VICTORIA LERDO DE TEJADA VS. JOSEPH PADULA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT JOSEPH PADULA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist re facts: 7, 9, 10, 11, 13, and 14. No response to additional facts submitted by Plaintiff. No objections to Plaintiffs evidence. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MAYFIELD et al VS. BALDWIN FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Hearing On The Issue Of The Good Faith Of A Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT ASHOK PATEL's Motion For Hearing On The Issue Of The Good Faith Settlement DENIED without prejudice, insufficient information regarding Tech-Bilt factors numbers 1, 2, 5, 6, and 7. Settling defendants have not provided adequate information or analysis to determine whether the settlement satisfies the standards of good faith set forth in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward Clyde & Associates (1995) 38 Cal.3d 488.). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BALWANTSINH THAKOR VS. THE INDUSTRY GROUP LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Balwantsinh Thakor'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication Regarding Duty To Deliver Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, line 7. Plaintiff Balwantsinh Thakor's Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication Regarding Duty To Deliver Possession GRANTED as to the action based on non-payment of rent. Plaintiff shifted its burden. Objection to paragraph 2 of Lipton declaration is sustained. Defendant failed to produce competent admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. January 16, 2015 letter did not extent the time to cure the operative notice and did not constitute waiver. It explicitly was based on CC 1719. = (501/REQ)
